name: zowe-mcp-vscode
description: Zowe MCP Server for z/OS - provides AI tools for data sets, jobs, and UNIX System Services.
author: Zowe

enumDescriptions: Connect to z/OS via SSH (Zowe Native). Configure connections in 'Native connections'.
Use local mock data for development and testing. A mock data directory will be configured automatically.
zoweMCP.backend.description: Backend to use for z/OS operations. Changes require running 'Developer: Reload Window' command.
zoweMCP.backend.markdownDescription: Backend to use for z/OS operations. Changes require running **Developer: Reload Window** command.

zoweMCP.nativeConnections.description: SSH connection specs (user@host or user@host:port) that define access to z/OS systems. Each entry is one connection (e.g. USERID@sys1.example.com). You can have multiple connections to the same z/OS system (e.g. different user IDs). Passwords are stored in the shared Zowe secret namespace (zowe.ssh.password.*).
zoweMCP.nativeConnections.markdownDescription: SSH connection specs (`user@host` or `user@host:port`) that define access to z/OS systems. Each entry is one connection (e.g. `USERID@sys1.example.com`). You can have multiple connections to the same z/OS system (e.g. different user IDs). Passwords are stored in the shared Zowe secret namespace (`zowe.ssh.password.*`).

zoweMCP.logLevel.description: Minimum log level for the Zowe MCP Server. Changes take effect immediately without restarting.

zoweMCP.installZoweNativeServerAutomatic.description: When true, automatically install the Zowe Native server on the host when "Server not found" is detected. Set to false to disable auto-install. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.
zoweMCP.installZoweNativeServerAutomatic.markdownDescription: When `true`, automatically install the Zowe Native server on the host when "Server not found" is detected. Set to `false` to disable auto-install. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.

zoweMCP.zoweNativeServerPath.patternErrorMessage: Must be a Unix-style path for z/OS UNIX System Services (e.g. ~/.zowe-server or /var/zowe-server)
zoweMCP.zoweNativeServerPath.description: Remote path on the z/OS host (UNIX System Services). Must start with / or ~. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.
zoweMCP.zoweNativeServerPath.markdownDescription: Remote path on the z/OS host (UNIX System Services). Must start with `/` or `~` (e.g. `~/.zowe-server` or `/var/zowe-server`). Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.

zoweMCP.nativeResponseTimeout.description: Response timeout in seconds for each Zowe Native (ZNP) request. Increase on overloaded systems to avoid timeouts. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.
zoweMCP.nativeResponseTimeout.markdownDescription: Response timeout in seconds for each Zowe Native (ZNP) request (e.g. list data sets, read data set). Increase on overloaded systems to avoid timeouts. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.

zoweMCP.mockDataDirectory.patternErrorMessage: Must not contain control characters. Use an absolute path for your OS (e.g. /path, C:\path, or ~/path).
zoweMCP.mockDataDirectory.description: Local absolute path to a mock data directory. Used when Backend is set to 'mock'. Use the 'Zowe MCP: Generate Mock Data' command to create one. Changes require running 'Developer: Reload Window' command.
zoweMCP.mockDataDirectory.markdownDescription: Local absolute path to a mock data directory (e.g. `/path`, `C:\\path`, or `~/path`). Used when **Backend** is set to **mock**. Use the **Zowe MCP: Generate Mock Data** command to create one. Changes require running **Developer: Reload Window** command.

zoweMCP.defaultMainframeMvsEncoding.patternErrorMessage: Must be a valid IBM encoding format (e.g. IBM-037, IBM-1047)
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeMvsEncoding.description: Default mainframe encoding (EBCDIC) for data set read/write (e.g. IBM-037, IBM-1047). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeMvsEncoding.markdownDescription: Default mainframe encoding (EBCDIC) for data set read/write (e.g. `IBM-037`, `IBM-1047`). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.

zoweMCP.defaultMainframeUssEncoding.patternErrorMessage: Must be a valid IBM encoding format (e.g. IBM-1047)
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeUssEncoding.description: Default mainframe encoding for USS file operations (e.g. IBM-1047). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeUssEncoding.markdownDescription: Default mainframe encoding for USS file operations (e.g. `IBM-1047`). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.

oneOf.description: Job card as list of lines (recommended; one line per array element).
Full job card as a single string (use \n for newlines).

zoweMCP.jobCards.description: JCL job cards per connection spec (key: user@host or user@host:port). Prefer array of lines, e.g. ["//{jobname}  JOB (ACCT),'{programmer}',CLASS=A,...", "/*JOBPARM S=*"]. Use placeholders {jobname} and {programmer}; submitJob substitutes them. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.
zoweMCP.jobCards.markdownDescription:
JCL job cards per connection spec. Key is `user@host` or `user@host:port`. **Prefer an array of lines** (one string per line), for example:

```json
"USER@zos.example.com": [
  "//{jobname}  JOB (ACCT),'{programmer}',CLASS=A,MSGCLASS=X,NOTIFY=&SYSUID",
  "/*JOBPARM S=*"
]
```

Use placeholders `{jobname}` and `{programmer}`; `submitJob` substitutes them. You can also use a single string with `\n` for newlines. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.

yaml.description: Absolute path to the CLI plugin YAML file (e.g. /path/to/endevor-tools.yaml).

connectionFile.description: Absolute path to a CliConnectionConfig JSON file with host/port/user/password/pluginParams. Omit for profile-only authentication.

descVariant.description: Description variant to use for the plugin's tools (default: intent).

zoweMCP.cliPlugins.description: CLI plugin bridge configurations. Each entry activates one plugin YAML as a set of MCP tools. For Endevor: point 'yaml' to the bundled endevor-tools.yaml in the extension's server directory, and optionally 'connectionFile' to a JSON with your Endevor connection details. Changes require restarting the MCP server (Reload Window).
zoweMCP.cliPlugins.markdownDescription:
CLI plugin bridge configurations. Each entry activates one plugin YAML as a set of MCP tools.

For Endevor, example entry:
```json
{
  "yaml": "/absolute/path/to/endevor-tools.yaml",
  "connectionFile": "/path/to/.endevor-connection.json",
  "descVariant": "intent"
}
```

The connection JSON file (`CliConnectionConfig`) can contain `host`, `port`, `user`, `password`, `protocol`, `basePath`, and `pluginParams` (e.g. `{"pluginProfile": "my-endevor-profile"}`). Omit `connectionFile` to rely on Zowe CLI profiles. Changes require **Developer: Reload Window**.

scripts.build:server: npm run build -w @zowe/mcp-server && npm run bundle:server
scripts.build:all: npm run build:server && npm run build
scripts.build-and-install: npm run package && npm run install-extension
